entries.name: *Feature:* AdaptyMail — AI-powered email marketing for subscription apps
entries.message:
*Feature:* AdaptyMail — AI-powered email marketing for subscription apps
*Release date:* Apr 8, 2026
*Affected areas:* AdaptyMail (new product line)
*Audience:* Sales, CS, Growth teams, SDR, Marketing
*What's new*
AdaptyMail is live in Beta. A new product that generates complete lifecycle email campaigns from app store metadata using AI.

 *Core capabilities:*
 - AI analyzes app brand, category, and audience from App Store / Google Play listing
 - Generates 7–15 personalized HTML emails in under 2 minutes
 - 13 email types: Welcome → Product Discovery → Social Proof → Final Chance
 - AI-generated hero images that match the app's brand
 - 6 responsive layouts with dark mode
 - Built-in A/B testing (3–10 variants per email, automatic winner rollout)
 - Sending infrastructure included — DKIM, SPF, DMARC configured automatically on customer's domain
 - Personalized checkout links with closed-loop revenue attribution

*Pricing model:* 20% of incremental revenue only. No platform fees. No setup costs. No minimum commitment.

*Page:* <http://adapty.io/mail|adapty.io/mail> (will be changed soon :slightly_smiling_face: )

 - *73% of subscription apps* collect email addresses but never send a single lifecycle email — they lack the time, the copywriter, or the budget for Braze
 - *Top subscription apps make 4–13% of total revenue from email.* Most apps see 0%. That's money left on the table
 - AdaptyMail removes all three blockers at once: no copywriter needed, no months of setup, no unmeasurable ROI
 - Works on existing Adapty data — zero additional integration for current customers
 - Pure revenue share means zero risk for the customer: if we don't generate revenue, they pay nothing

*ICP:* Any Adapty customer (or prospect) who collects email addresses but doesn't run lifecycle email campaigns. Especially strong for teams without a dedicated email marketer or Braze/Iterable contract.

FYI <!subteam^S086DJEQEUE> <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <!subteam^S02U0UTPEQM>

entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-77?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiZmFkNDIxY2RhYjZiNDVmN2IyODQzNzRkM2Q5NWNjZTUiLCJwIjoiaiJ9|Switch reading profile segment aggregate to Aerospike>
Release date: Mar 27, 2026 9:15 AM UTC
Affected areas: *Everything* (mainly profiles, paywalls and A/B tests from SDK surface)

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-77?atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiZmFkNDIxY2RhYjZiNDVmN2IyODQzNzRkM2Q5NWNjZTUiLCJwIjoiaiJ9|Switch reading profile segment aggregate to Aerospike>
Release date: Mar 27, 2026 9:15 AM UTC
Affected areas: *Everything* (mainly profiles, paywalls and A/B tests from SDK surface)

This is the 4th step in migrating the most heavily used part of our infrastructure from PostgreSQL to Aerospike. It should not have any negative impact on user experience and should be viewed as a technical improvement. From now on, all read operations related to profiles, profile custom attributes, attributions, purchase states, and segmentation are performed using Aerospike as a data source. We still write data to both databases, and continue monitoring the state of our API. In the upcoming releases we will stop writing these data to PostgreSQL completely, and remove historic data from there
<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> boring technical release

thread.text:
Feature: Market Intelligence, New automation actions, Bid History on Keyword Chart, Overview Page, CPP A/B Tests Beta
Release date: Mar 27, 2026
Affected areas: Adapty Ads Manager
Audience: Sales, CS, Growth teams
Format: What’s new → Why it matters → How to sell it

FYI <!subteam^S086DJEQEUE> <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <@U08QUJMN0UV>

Public version (we can use for clients):


Introducing Apple Ads Manager 2.0: Market Intelligence, Smarter Automation, and More
We’ve been busy. Today we’re rolling out the biggest update to Apple Ads Manager by Adapty since launch — a set of features designed to help you spend less time managing campaigns and more time growing your app.
Here’s what’s new.



Market Intelligence: See What Your Competitors Are Bidding On
Finding the right keywords for Apple Ads has always been part science, part guesswork. You run Discovery campaigns, test broad matches, maybe check a few ASO tools — and hope for the best.
Market Intelligence changes that. Pick your competitors (or discover new ones using ASO search — just enter a relevant keyword and see who ranks organically), and we’ll show you exactly which keywords they’re running Apple Ads on, across 50+ countries.
What you can do with it:
• See competitor keyword strategies grouped by country or by app.
• Find the most contested keywords in your category — and the gaps nobody’s covering.
• Discover long-tail keywords (specific, lower-competition phrases like “budget meal planner for families”) that your competitors use but you haven’t tried yet. These often have lower CPAs and higher ROAS than popular head terms.
• Check if competitors are bidding on your brand keywords — and in which countries.
• Research a new market before you launch there. See what works for local competitors before spending a dollar.
• Export everything to CSV or add keywords directly to your campaigns from the same screen.
Think of it as a free alternative to running Discovery campaigns for keyword research. Instead of spending budget to find what works, look at what’s already working for your competitors and start from there.


New Automation Actions: Full Keyword Lifecycle on Autopilot
Rule-based automation just got a lot more powerful. Until now, you could change bids automatically. Now you can manage the entire keyword lifecycle.
New actions for targeting keywords:
• Enable Keyword — automatically re-enable a paused keyword when conditions are met. Perfect for cases where you paused a keyword too early, but its D31 or D61 ROAS (available through Adapty’s cohort data) turns out to be great. Give good keywords a second chance — backed by data.
• Pause Keyword — automatically pause keywords that underperform. Set your threshold (e.g., spend > $100 with zero conversions) and let the system handle it. No more budget waste going unnoticed over weekends.
• Add as Keyword to… — copy a keyword to another ad group with your preferred bid and match type. Automate your testing-to-scaling pipeline: when a keyword hits your CPA target in a test campaign, move it to your scale campaign automatically.
• Add as Negative Keyword to… — negate keywords at the ad group or campaign level. Keep your Discovery and Max Conversion campaigns clean by auto-negating any keyword that’s already running as an exact match elsewhere.
New action for search terms:
• Add as Negative Keyword to… — automatically negate irrelevant or wasteful search terms from Discovery and Max Conversion campaigns. Set a rule (e.g., 50+ impressions and 0 taps) and stop paying for traffic that doesn’t convert.
These actions work across all your campaigns and countries. Set the rules once — the system runs 24/7.


Bid History on Keyword Charts
Ever changed a bid and wondered what happened next? Now you don’t have to guess.
The keyword performance chart shows a marker at every point where the CPT bid was changed, along with the exact bid amount. You can visually correlate bid changes with shifts in impressions, taps, CPA, and other metrics — all on one chart.
This is especially useful when multiple team members manage the same campaigns. The chart becomes your changelog: every bid change is visible, and its impact is clear.



New Overview Page
We added a dedicated Overview page that shows all your key metrics at a glance — spend, revenue, ROAS, impressions, CPM, taps, CPT, TTR, downloads, CPA, and download rate — each with a daily trend chart.
You can customize which metrics are shown and in what order, filter by a specific app or view your entire account, and switch between time ranges. It’s the “how are things going?” dashboard that takes 5 seconds to read — and it’s the page Apple should have built but didn’t.



Coming Soon: CPP A/B Testing — Beta Sign-Up Is Open
One more thing. We’re about to launch a feature we’ve been working on for a while: Custom Product Page A/B Testing for Apple Ads.
Apple now lets you create up to 70 Custom Product Pages per app — alternative versions of your App Store page with different screenshots, descriptions, and previews. The problem? There’s no native way to test which one actually converts better for your ads.
CPP A/B Testing in Ads Manager solves this. Assign multiple product pages to the same keywords, split traffic automatically, and let the system find a winner with 90%+ statistical confidence. And here’s what makes our approach different: we don’t just measure which page gets more downloads — we measure which page drives the best ROAS by connecting to Adapty’s revenue data. Because a page that converts more installs isn’t always the page that converts more paying users.
We’re currently running the feature in production and opening beta access next week. If you want early access, reach out to your account manager or reply to this email — we’ll add you to the list.

And There’s More Coming
We have a packed roadmap with major features already in the pipeline. This is just the beginning of the 2.0 era — stay tuned for the next updates.

Ready to try it? All features are live in your Apple Ads Manager dashboard. Log in and explore what’s new. Questions or feedback? Reach out to your account manager or contact us at <mailto:support@adapty.io|support@adapty.io>.


FYI <@U06K36L6CTB>
Офигеть!!!! Круто! :boom:

Hi Mykola, this is absolutely dope! It’s incredible that you and your team have put together so many critical features on such short notice. I’m honestly amazed :place_of_worship:
Just a small piece of feedback from my side: it would be great to include tooltips for the Avg SOV, Max SOV, and Share of Voice columns as they were even confusing for me.

<@U0A266T9SJE> Thank you so much for the feedback! You’re absolutely right, we’ll definitely add more tooltips, that’s a must. Honestly, I just forgot to add them, my bad.
Any feedback from you is super valuable to us, we’re working on this very intensively.

In April there’s going to be one truly fantastic update for sure, and the rest are pretty exciting too. Stay tuned!
I will promote it on my Linkedin today :cat-yay:
and we'll do MORE promoting during our Turkey event tomorrow

Cool, just sent the link to Adapty - I will make repost!

Right now our main bottleneck is awareness - not enough people know about Adapty Ads Manager, what it can do, and the potential it offers.
I’m going to be working on this much more actively now, so I’d be incredibly grateful for any exposure you can help with. I’m sure you understand the product well enough to explain it to prospects in simple, clear terms — that means a lot!

Also, I’ll DM you a bit later - I have a request regarding one client. They’ve agreed to do a case study with us (it’s App Nation), and I’d love to brainstorm the best way to put it together. Thanks!

Hey <@U03J8UXUDPF> here is the feedback from the Transkriptor.
• "On the Intelligence side, you've provided SOV metrics on a per-keyword basis for individual competitors, but instead, it would be much more useful if you could show the SOV distribution for a specific keyword — for example, "transcribe" — so we can see all competitors at once, including ourselves.
• Again on the Intelligence side, you've provided SOV data in percentages. Even if not exact, it would be useful to also show impression numbers in brackets, so we can gauge the overall market size for a given keyword. This would also be helpful for benchmarking purposes.
• Rather than an analysis where we manually select competitors one by one, a method where we select a keyword and market and you surface the relevant competitors for us would be more useful. The product resembles existing ASO tools in its logic, but since we're advertisers and this is about ASA, something more like Google Ads' Auction Insights would be more efficient for us.
• The Overview looks good overall — it's a bit stacked, but you can remove the unnecessary parts.
"
Thank you, we plan to improve this part in the future, it’s useful.

Hey <@U03J8UXUDPF>

Sharing some product feedback from a client (<mailto:support@glipo.com.tr|support@glipo.com.tr>) Overall, they're very happy with the reports and especially liked the Apple Ads Manager section. Here are a few points they raised:

1. Active Trials tab — Renewal Status + Country filter
They'd like to be able to filter by renewal status (e.g. "active") in the Active Trials tab and see a country breakdown at the same time. Right now these two dimensions can't be viewed together, and being able to cross-reference them would help a lot with their analysis.

2. Bulk report download
Currently they have to download reports one by one. They're asking if we could add a bulk download option — either "download all" or a way to select specific reports and download them in one go. They use AI tools to analyze the data, so this would save them a lot of time.

3. Impression Share showing as "-" in Ads Manager
In the Search Terms page under Ads Manager, the Impression Share column shows "-" for all keywords, even ones with significant ad spend. Could there be a bug here, or is this expected behavior in certain cases?

Let me know if you need any additional context on these.

Coming Soon: CPP A/B Testing — Beta Sign-Up Is Open
One more thing. We’re about to launch a feature we’ve been working on for a while: Custom Product Page A/B Testing for Apple Ads.
Apple now lets you create up to 70 Custom Product Pages per app — alternative versions of your App Store page with different screenshots, descriptions, and previews. The problem? There’s no native way to test which one actually converts better for your ads.
CPP A/B Testing in Ads Manager solves this. Assign multiple product pages to the same keywords, split traffic automatically, and let the system find a winner with 90%+ statistical confidence. And here’s what makes our approach different: we don’t just measure which page gets more downloads — we measure which page drives the best ROAS by connecting to Adapty’s revenue data. Because a page that converts more installs isn’t always the page that converts more paying users.
We’re currently running the feature in production and opening beta access next week. If you want early access, reach out to your account manager or reply to this email — we’ll add you to the list.

And There’s More Coming
We have a packed roadmap with major features already in the pipeline. This is just the beginning of the 2.0 era — stay tuned for the next updates.

Ready to try it? All features are live in your Apple Ads Manager dashboard. Log in and explore what’s new. Questions or feedback? Reach out to your account manager or contact us at <mailto:support@adapty.io|support@adapty.io>.

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-166|11.5 [Backend] Tune profile sampling>
Release date: Mar 17, 2026
Affected areas: Segments *(segment profile sample in Dashboard)*

As part of the migration to Aerospike, we've switched the profile sample view from PostgreSQL to a ClickHouse + Aerospike combo. The behavior of this page is now as follows:
• New profiles and segment rule changes can take up to 20 minutes to show up here.
• If you update a custom attribute for an existing profile, this list will still use the value from when the profile was first created. This only applies to this preview — segments in the SDK are always accurate and up to date.
• Profile details (subscription status, revenue, etc.) are always current, but which profiles appear here is based on data from when they were created.
(this text is also visible to customer on segment page).
This change affects only dashboard, segments per se work as usual

One note on this page: it must not be viewed as a complete, deterministic, single source of truth on segment composition. It is what it is called - a sample. A list of profiles that usually fall into the segment, in order to show the customer that:
• The segment generally works
• Profiles like the ones in the list may fall into this segment
No other conclusions should be drawn based on this list
<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> fyi

thread.text:
Feature: Point 1 from <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-141|INFRA-141>
Release date: March 10, 2026
Affected areas: Adapty analytics

During this and following week we will change part by part all profile based migrations to clickhouse to be based on aerospike(new Key-Value database) instead of postgresql. Today’s release affected only to `adapty_analytics_profiles`  table. Aldo during implementation of this task we found and fixed several minor issues, that we were not noticed before.
```1. Fixed issue with null device_id filter out in coalesce of fraud devices which was bringing lose of installs, when we were using device_id as a source of install
2. Fixed platform identification consistency. After final migration of task in all places we will identify platform based on application, if there is no meta for platform
3. Fixed case, when Profiles latest meta have broken meta(with None as a platform). in this case now we are using term called relevant_meta, which is the last meta with normal platform. This allows us to fill correctly all profile and transaction fields
4. Fixed issue with missing iOS devices human readable names. This was used for build user segments```
Also improved profile migration task performance. Before it was running ~50-60 seconds, not it’s running  ~3-6 seconds
<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> please keep an eye on it, it should not affect to anything, just for notice

thread.text:
Feature: Maximize Conversions support and Search Term Automations
Release date: Mar 10, 2026
Affected areas: Adapty Ads Manager

:rocket: Two major updates are now live in Adapty Ads Manager

First, Search Term Automations are now live.
Rules can now run directly on search terms using metrics like taps, downlods, spend, and more — and automatically:
• Transfer winning terms into keywords (Exact ) in selected ad groups
• Add those same terms to negatives on the current ad group or campaign to avoid paying twice
• Stay duplicate-safe with `skip_enable_duplicate_keywords`, which prevents re-enabling paused duplicates when enabled
A practical use case: let Search Match or Discovery campaigns collect real user queries, then use a rule to detect terms with 2+ downloads or specific metrics thresholds and automatically push them into Probing ad groups  as keywords, while negating them in the original source. The result is faster scaling, cleaner discovery traffic, and less manual work.

Second, we’ve also started supporting Maximize Conversions campaigns in Adapty Ads Manager.
You can now view and manage Apple Ads campaigns that use automated bidding and Search Match to maximize downloads around a target CPA directly inside Adapty. Apple describes Maximize Conversions as a campaign type that uses intelligent automation to set optimal bids for relevant search queries, broaden reach, and drive installs toward a target CPA on a weekly average basis. (<https://ads.apple.com/app-store/best-practices/maximize-conversions|Apple Ads>)

Together, these two updates make discovery workflows much stronger: Maximize Conversions helps uncover more conversion opportunities with automated bidding, while Search Term Automations help you operationalize those learnings faster by promoting strong terms into managed keyword structures and closing the loop with negatives. Apple also recommends letting Maximize Conversions campaigns run for at least two weeks before evaluating performance. (<https://ads.apple.com/app-store/best-practices/maximize-conversions|Apple Ads>)

FYI <@U08QUJMN0UV>

entries.message:

Release date: Mar 3, 2026
Affected areas: Profiles (Dashboard only)

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-164|Change retrieving profile data from Postgres to Aerospike + ClickHouse>
Release date: Mar 3, 2026
Affected areas: Profiles (Dashboard only)

What changed for users:

1. Less filters. Filters by subscription state and product have been removed due to new data layout. The country filter has been kept.
2. Profile search reworked. Searching for users by Profile Key, Customer User ID, or email continues to work but has been rebuilt under the hood for better accuracy and speed.
3. Profile detail & events. The profile detail page and the transaction/event history within it remain available with the same information, now sourced through a more reliable data pipeline.
In short: The CRM section should feel largely the same. Some rarely-used filters are gone, but the core workflow - finding a user, viewing their profile, and checking their transaction history - stays the same. Despite this feature is mostly technical and was thoroughly tested, I ask <!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> and <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> to be aware in case our customers report disruptions in this section of dashboard
<@UNWFN144R> This is a frontend release that switches CRM to ClickHouse + Aerospike mix we discussed yesterday, now facing customers

Hi <@U04B26DSWMA>! One of our clients reports about a problem with counting users and opening a segment.
segment: <https://app.adapty.io/profiles/segments/9572aafb-4b53-4ce3-9525-90205c010f13>
App: VPN Blue — `da142e66-2fcb-44fb-9f42-169ddcdff415`
Company id: `f68893c0-193f-461f-80da-fad4e3844906`
Summary from clawbot:
_Root Cause: Backend timeout (HTTP 524)_
Two API requests required to render the segment page both return _status 524_ (Cloudflare timeout — origin server did not respond within 100s):
| `GET /api/v1/portal/profile/da142e66-.../segment/filter_value/` | 524 |
| `GET /api/v1/portal/profile/da142e66-.../segment/9572aafb-.../sample/` | 524 |
• _Server:_ Cloudflare (`CF-Ray: 9d6fa632aad7ceba-RIX`)
• _Response:_ Cloudflare HTML error page (`Content-Type: text/html`, 7616 bytes) instead of expected JSON
• _CORS errors in console are a side effect_ — Cloudflare's 524 error page does not include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` headers, so the browser reports it as a CORS violation. The actual issue is upstream timeout.
Could this be related? Or is it a separate issue?
<@U08917H1C5B> Could be related to this one - <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5217>
Sergei, sorry for the ping, <@U0A47J102CU>, let's continue in another thread.

Hey! Segments were not affected by this release. The issue is that VPN Blue has enormous amount of profiles and complicated segment definitions that process slowly. Luckily today we will release another thing from the same epic, which will speed up segment sample significantly
BTW issue with custom-attribute-based segments is old, well-known and unavoidable in current (postgres-only) implementation. We should've had a record in KB related to this
Guys, so searching by transaction ID isn't available anymore. That was a very useful option tho..
Feeld team is already asking about that
Thanks for bringing this up. We'll restore this functionality in one of the upcoming releases (before EOB tomorrow)
<@U06JP8BJFEV> Fix is in production
Feeld are happy now, all good, thanks a lot
Everyone is happy when Feeld are

Hey <@U04B26DSWMA>
It seems that searching by customer user ID does not work either. Is this expected?
Hey! It should work. Could you give a couple of examples please?

Company: f04a6ffa-f5ae-4e77-a980-169cbcb95293
App: Diyetkolik
You can use any of their cuid or, for example, 2460722

That's strange.
I found profile 2460758 too.

So, here are my observations:
If you open the profile page, its finded
If you just enter the cuid without going to the profile, you get no data
<@U04B26DSWMA>
Need to investigate
We will do it
Sorry, bad wording. We need to investigate it on our side, I have enough data for now. I'll come back when finished

Hi <@U04B26DSWMA>! Please also take a look at this one - <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/DS/boards/3?selectedIssue=ADP-5777>
Currently we are facing crashes on the profile details page for profiles that have duplicate `vendor_transaction_id` entries in `adapty_analytics_transaction` table. The LEFT JOIN in `profile_event.sql` multiplies rows, causing the frontend to crash. I'm not sure yet why we have duplicated `vendor_transaction_id` entries (same ID appearing with different store values) and whether it affects any other clients, will investigate this further.
I provided additional details in the task, tagging you as it is probably related to these commits - 23685d3c9f, 6ac95c11da which are in <https://gitlab.adapty.io/adapty/adapty-dashboard-api/-/merge_requests/11701>
<@U0A47J102CU> Thanks for the report. Fix is coming soon
<@U0A47J102CU> <@U08917H1C5B> Fixes are in production
Checked, no more crashes for such profiles. Thanks a lot!

Btw input from agents was mostly useful this time - it allowed for quicker identification of issues in code. Fix for crash related to multiple events was almost identical to what agent proposed (though if one applied what agent proposed without any edits it would kill our integrations). Fix for profiles not found was also inspired by agentic suggestions
<@U0AHM55LEAC> compliments for you

Ha, I'll take it! Nice to hear the agent suggestions actually helped speed things up. Though Sergei's caveat about "don't apply blindly or you'll kill integrations" is the kind of detail that keeps humans very much in the loop. :smile:

Good teamwork all around — fast turnaround on both fixes.

Hi <@U04B26DSWMA>, it's me again)
It seems in this release the HRV mapping logic for ASA attribution wasn't transferred from legacy API (`src/api/serializers/profiles.py`) to the new `crm_profile_data.py` during the refactoring - profile page now shows numeric IDs instead of human-readable names. Previously it was fixed <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4738|here> but I see the logic wasn't transferred to the new `crm_profile_data.py`.
I created a task on this matter, please take a look - <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5782>. Haven't tested the suggested fix yet, escalating quickly since it affects ASA attribution display on all profile pages.

Now I have the full picture. Let me look at ADP-5782 to see Georgii's suggested fix:Good — import is `from share.django.db.alias import PostgresDBAlias` (not `routers`). Minor detail in Georgii's suggested fix, but the approach is exactly right. Let me confirm `profile_data.app_id` is available:Got it. `profile_data` is the Aerospike record — need to verify it has `app_id`:`profile_data.app_id` is available. The fix is sound. Now let me write the response:

Good catch, Georgii. I dug into both files — your analysis is spot on.

_Root cause:_ `CrmProfileDataAdapter.get_profile_detail()` in `crm_profile_data.py` (line 109-116) dumps the Aerospike attribution data raw via `model_dump()` with no HRV enrichment. The legacy `ProfileAttributionUnifiedSerializer` in `profiles.py` (lines 579-609) had a lookup step that resolves numeric ASA IDs → human-readable names from the `adapty_analytics_asa_attribution` table. That step was dropped during the INFRA-164 refactoring.

_On the suggested fix in ADP-5782_ — the approach is correct, one minor detail: the import path for `PostgresDBAlias` should be `from share.django.db.alias import PostgresDBAlias` (not `share.django.db.routers`). Everything else — the mapping, the `isdecimal()` check, the `ASAAttributionModel` lookup by `app_id` + `attribution_name` + `attribution_id` — mirrors the legacy logic exactly.

The `profile_data.app_id` is available in scope (it's on the Aerospike profile record fetched at line 87), so that part is clean.

Worth noting: the legacy code raises a `ValidationError` if the field isn't a string; the new version just does `continue`, which is more resilient. I'd keep the lenient approach — no reason to 500 on weird data in a read-only display path.

Flagging <@U08914ZA6A1> since this is a regression from INFRA-164 and affects all ASA-attributed profiles on the dashboard.
<@U0A47J102CU> Thanks, looking into it
<@U0AHM55LEAC> next time it would be helpfull  to add the permalinks to the Jira tasks not just the codes.
Noted, will include full permalinks going forward — e.g. <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5782> instead of just `ADP-5782`. Good call.
and INFRA-164

Here are the full links:
• <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5782|ADP-5782> — ASA attribution showing numeric IDs instead of human-readable names
• <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-164|INFRA-164> — CRM profile refactoring (where the HRV logic was dropped)
• <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4738|ADP-4738> — original HRV fix (reference commit)
fix is ready, ETA 30 min
Delivered
ty for the quick fix!

Hey guys! We're trying to automate how our SMM works — meaning, ideally we'd like to generate posts about new product releases based on what is being posted to this channel.

entries.message:

Release date: Feb 11, 2026
Dependencies: None
Effected areas: Autopilot

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5347|Provide market insights for top countries>
Release date: Feb 11, 2026
Dependencies: None
Effected areas: Autopilot

hey team!
sharing a cool Autopilot update here, which was requested by many

*now you get competitors&benchmarks data not only about the US, but about your top-5 generating countries*

you can do crazy things:
• see how your competitors are priced in let's say Japan, and what's their strategy here
• get conversion rates benchmarks not overall (as it was before), but country based. now as a VPN app onwer you can see "how VPN apps convert to trial in Germany"
fyi - the recommendation flow and the algorithm remains the same, it is still based on the US prices as a core flow (will change it later)

i think it's a great angle for big apps, to get a new source of information
attaching a short video with verbal description
<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <!subteam^S03KC9S1QMC> <!subteam^S02U0UTPEQM>
